message.content:
System Binary Proxy Execution: Rundll32 is a technique where adversaries exploit the Windows utility rundll32.exe to execute malicious code indirectly. This method can bypass security tools that may not monitor rundll32.exe due to allowlists or false positives. Rundll32.exe is typically used to execute DLL payloads, Control Panel Item files (.cpl), and scripts like JavaScript. Adversaries may obscure malicious code by manipulating DLL function names or using Masquerading techniques.

The impact of this technique includes evasion of security defenses, persistence, and execution of arbitrary code. Common exploitation methods involve using rundll32.exe to load DLLs, execute scripts, and maintain persistence through registry modifications. Notable threat groups such as APT28, APT29, and Lazarus Group have utilized this technique for various malicious activities, including initial infection, payload execution, and lateral movement.

Mitigation strategies include monitoring and analyzing rundll32.exe usage, implementing strict allowlists, and employing behavior-based detection mechanisms. Regularly auditing registry keys and system binaries for unauthorized modifications can also help in identifying and preventing abuse of rundll32.exe.
